relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Judah's lot begins at the southern edge near Edom and Zin.
The Salt Sea opens the southern boundary.
The line travels through Akrabbim, Zin, Kadesh-barnea, Hezron, Addar, and Karka.
Azmon and the wadi of Egypt carry the border to the sea.
Salt Sea and Jordan define the east and begin the north.
Beth-hoglah, Beth-arabah, and Bohan's stone mark the ascent.
Achor, Gilgal, Adummim, En-shemesh, and En-rogel continue the remembered line.
The boundary climbs Hinnom beside Jebusite Jerusalem and the Rephaim valley.
Nephtoah, Ephron, and Kiriath-jearim carry it westward.
Seir, Chesalon, Beth-shemesh, and Timnah shape another turn.
Ekron, Shikkeron, Baalah, and Jabneel lead to the coast.
The Great Sea completes Judah's allotted perimeter.
Caleb receives Hebron inside Judah's inheritance.
Sheshai, Ahiman, and Talmai are displaced from their home.
Caleb turns from Hebron toward Debir.
Achsah's marriage is offered as a reward for conquest.
Othniel captures the city and receives Achsah as wife.
Achsah creates space to ask for more than the arrangement gave her.
She names the need clearly: dry land requires upper and lower springs.
The city register begins as Judah's clan inheritance.
Kabzeel, Eder, and Jagur open the southern list.
Kinah, Dimonah, and Adadah follow.
Kedesh, Hazor, and Ithnan are named.
Ziph, Telem, and Bealoth enter the register.
Hazor-hadattah and Kerioth-hezron preserve layered place names.
Amam, Shema, and Moladah are listed.
Hazar-gaddah, Heshmon, and Beth-pelet are listed.
Hazar-shual, Beer-sheba, and Biziothiah are listed.
Baalah, Iim, and Ezem are listed.
Eltolad, Chesil, and Hormah are listed.
Ziklag, Madmannah, and Sansannah are listed.
The southern group closes with Lebaoth, Shilhim, Ain, and Rimmon.
The lowland list begins with Eshtaol, Zorah, and Ashnah.
Zanoah, En-gannim, Tappuah, and Enam follow.
Jarmuth, Adullam, Socoh, and Azekah are named.
Shaaraim, Adithaim, Gederah, and Gederothaim close fourteen lowland cities.
Zenan, Hadashah, and Migdal-gad begin another cluster.
Dilean, Mizpeh, and Joktheel follow.
Lachish, Bozkath, and Eglon return as allotted towns after conquest.
Cabbon, Lahmam, and Chitlish are named.
Gederoth, Beth-dagon, Naamah, and Makkedah complete the group.
Libnah, Ether, and Ashan begin nine more cities.
Iphtah, Ashnah, and Nezib follow.
Keilah, Achzib, and Mareshah close that cluster.
Ekron appears with its dependent settlements.
The coastal register moves from Ekron toward Ashdod.
Ashdod and Gaza are allotted despite continuing Philistine presence.
The hill-country cities begin with Shamir, Jattir, and Socoh.
Dannah and Debir are named.
Anab, Eshtemoh, and Anim follow.
Goshen, Holon, and Giloh complete eleven towns.
Arab, Dumah, and Eshan begin another group.
Janim, Beth-tappuah, and Aphekah follow.
Humtah, Hebron, and Zior close nine cities.
Maon, Carmel, Ziph, and Juttah are named.
Jezreel, Jokdeam, and Zanoah follow.
Kain, Gibeah, and Timnah close ten towns.
Halhul, Beth-zur, and Gedor begin six cities.
Maarath, Beth-anoth, and Eltekon complete the preserved Masoretic group.
Kiriath-jearim and Rabbah stand as a two-city cluster.
The wilderness list begins with Beth-arabah, Middin, and Secacah.
Nibshan, City of Salt, and En-gedi complete the desert towns.
Jebusites remain in Jerusalem, refusing the fiction that allotment equals total possession.

contemplative_note: Joshua 15 is mostly map, but Achsah interrupts the map with a practical truth: land without water is not life. Her demand for springs exposes the difference between receiving territory and receiving what makes a future possible. The closing Jebusite presence exposes another difference: allotment is not ownership of every person already there.
